question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the waitForDatabase() method does not check the database server version, allowing a connection to an older PostgreSQL version that lacks support for certain fee schedule constraints (e.g., CHECK constraints on bigint columns), enabling insertion of fee values outside the design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the retry loop does not limit the number of connection attempts (only the total time via deadline), enabling a resource exhaustion attack where each connection attempt to a slow rogue database consumes a thread and file descriptor, eventually causing the JVM to run out of resources before connecting to the legitimate database with correct fee schedule data?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the jdbcUrl is passed as a plain String without any SSRF protection, to supply a URL pointing to an internal network database (e.g., jdbc:postgresql://169.254.169.254/mirrornode) that contains pre-modified fee schedule entries, exploiting cloud metadata service IP ranges to bypass network controls?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the log.info() call logs 'Successfully connected to database' without logging the actual jdbcUrl used, making it impossible to detect in audit logs when the application connected to a rogue database with modified fee schedule data instead of the legitimate production database?
